title: Inspect the existing MBP/local launcher setup in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public. Focus on scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*, and any prompt-install related gaps for making /kernel and /vote available in Codex on MBP. Return: 1) current behavior, 2) c...
summary_text: 1) Current behavior - Launcher install only handles executable and shell bootstrap: - `scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh` installs `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` to `~/bin/kernel`. - It also appends (or verifies) sourcing of `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh` in `~/.zshrc`. - No prompt files are installed there. - `/kernel` dispatch is partially wired: - `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh` only wraps `codex` commands and routes to the `kernel` command when `~/bin/kernel-root` exists. - `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` executes runtime flow, including `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint`, `codex-kernel-guard`, and fallback to `codex-prompt-launch kernel`. - Prompt bootstrap checks exist but are not installation steps: - `scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` checks repo prompts and optionally checks global prompt files if they already exist; it does not create/install them. - `scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh` only checks repo-local vote prompt coverage; it does not ensure global installation. - `/vote` is documented as a local continuation prompt in `CODEX.md`, but installer path does not set it up globally for Codex. 2) Concrete gap - The MBP local installer does not provision `/kernel` and `/vote` prompt definitions into the user-facing prompt path (`$HOME/.codex/prompts`) or equivalent Codex registration mechanism. - Practically, `/kernel` and `/vote` availability in Codex depends on repo-local context/manual prompt loading rather than a guaranteed installer action, so new MBP shells are not guaranteed to have those slash commands available. - Secondary gap: launcher assumptions reference helper binaries (`orchestrator-entrypoint-hint`, `kernel-root`, `codex-prompt-launch`) that are not installed by this script, so `/kernel` behavior can silently degrade to partial/fallback paths. 3) Recommended minimal implementation - Extend `scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh` with an explicit, idempotent prompt-install step: - create `${HOME}/.codex/prompts` if missing. - install/symlink: - `kernel.md` and `k.md` - `vote.md` and `v.md` from repo `.codex/prompts/` into `${HOME}/.codex/prompts/`. - only overwrite if file missing or `--force-prompts` is passed. - Add an explicit check in installer/check flow (new `--check-prompts` or by extending `--check`) that validates: - both prompt files exist in expected global location, - and optionally that they resolve to current repo versions. - Document the MBP bootstrap sequence clearly (`README.md` and/or `CODEX.md`) to include prompt installation so `/kernel` and `/vote` are guaranteed available after setup. - Optional safety tweak: emit a clear warning in `install-kernel-launchers.sh` if expected helper binaries are missing instead of relying on implicit fallbacks.
